summary: Clear soundboard capture of the 2‑21‑91 Oakland show, highlighted by a smoking Help > Slip > Franklin’s, stellar Eyes out of Space and one of the best Slipknot jams.
review: The SBD recording is clean with excellent instrument placement and only a single minor glitch noted. Musically the concert shines especially in the second set – the Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s sequence, the soaring Eyes out of Space and a magnificent Slipknot make it a must‑listen for 1991 fans. Vince’s organ work adds extra flavor while Jerry’s guitar is on fire throughout.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high 4‑star reviews (plus a few 5‑star) praise both the performance and the soundboard quality; only one 3‑star mentions an average overall impression, giving a strong positive bias.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp mix with good placement of each band member; reviewers note it as a “nice board” and “very nice SBD”. One reviewer mentions a single minor glitch, but no major issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid with a few surprising moments; second set tight and jam‑heavy, flowing seamlessly from Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s into Terrapin, Space and the unforgettable Eyes out of Space.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for ripping it up on guitar, especially during the Eyes solo and the Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s jam; described as playing his heart out.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince's organ/keyboard work is highlighted – heavy at times but generally solid, with great runs on Franklin’s, Memphis Blues and the Slipknot sequence.
